relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: YHWH speaks camp boundaries and repair.
Certain ritual conditions are moved outside the sanctuary-centered camp.
Male and female alike are included in the camp-boundary rule.
Israel follows the instruction.
YHWH speaks restitution.
Any man or woman who harms another acts unfaithfully against YHWH.
Confession is joined to return of principal and an added fifth.
When no human redeemer exists, the restitution passes through priestly custody.
Holy contributions remain with the priestly service.
What is given becomes the priest's due.
YHWH speaks the ordeal of jealousy.
A husband's suspicion brings his wife into a patriarchal ritual process.
The accusation concerns concealed adultery without witness.
Jealousy may arise whether guilt exists or not.
A barley offering of remembrance accompanies the accusation.
The priest stands the woman before YHWH.
Holy water and sanctuary dust form the ordeal's sign.
Her hair is loosened and the bitter water is placed before her.
She is told that innocence releases her from the curse.
The oath then names the accusation of unfaithfulness.
The curse language is severe and body-centered.
The woman answers the imposed oath, Amen, amen.
The written curse is dissolved into the water.
She is made to drink the bitter water.
The jealousy offering is waved before YHWH.
Its memorial portion rises, and the ordeal proceeds.
The text imagines bodily consequence if guilt is present.
If innocent, she is released and remains able to conceive.
This is the ancient instruction of jealous suspicion.
The husband's jealousy activates the priestly procedure.
The chapter closes with an unequal assignment of liability that must remain under ethical critique.

contemplative_note: Numbers 5 moves from material restitution into a deeply patriarchal ordeal. Its enduring ethical direction is not reenactment but the opposite: reject private retaliation, coercion, jealousy-as-proof, and abuse; insist on truth, evidence, dignity, and repair.
